Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 905 upvotes, 131 comments
  2. MariaDB innovation: vector index performance 21 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. The Enterprise Context Layer 20 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Defeat as Method 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Isotopic Evidence for a Cold and Distant Origin of Interstellar Object 3I/Atlas 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 174 upvotes, 56 comments
  7. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 162 upvotes, 52 comments
  8. PgAdmin 4 9.13 with AI Assistant Panel 74 upvotes, 20 comments
  9. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 112 upvotes, 37 comments
  10. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 37 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 242 upvotes, 100 comments
  12. I put my whole life into a single database 353 upvotes, 171 comments
  13. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 29 upvotes, 10 comments
  14. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 373 upvotes, 229 comments
  15. Throwing away 18 months of code and starting over 28 upvotes, 10 comments
  16. Open Weights Isn't Open Training 26 upvotes, 9 comments
  17. How many options fit into a boolean? 43 upvotes, 19 comments
  18. Bypassing Apache Fop PostScript Escaping to Reach GhostScript 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Meta acquires Moltbook 253 upvotes, 162 comments
  20. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  21. Billion-Parameter Theories 37 upvotes, 20 comments
  22. Rebasing in Magit 141 upvotes, 102 comments
  23. A new Oracle Solaris Common Build Environment (CBE) release 41 upvotes, 25 comments
  24. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 179 upvotes, 146 comments
  25. RFC 454545 – Human Em Dash Standard 90 upvotes, 67 comments
  26. Levels of Agentic Engineering 20 upvotes, 11 comments
  27. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 307 upvotes, 321 comments
  28. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 37 upvotes, 38 comments
  29. I built a programming language using Claude Code 54 upvotes, 67 comments
  30. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 73 upvotes, 234 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
